files.summary: CLI entry point using Commander — commands: init (analyze + LLM generate CLAUDE.md), analyze (run analyzers only), set-llm (configure LLM provider). Handles LLM config, prompt building, and markdown extraction.

LLM provider abstraction — supports Claude (API + CLI), OpenAI API, Ollama CLI, and Gemini CLI. Contains the CLAUDE.md system prompt. Returns either a response or a prompt string for CLI-based generation.
LLM config management — load/save config from ~/.mason/config.json. Supports providers: claude, gemini, openai, ollama. Includes CLI detection for claude/gemini.

MCP server definition using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk — exposes tools: full_analysis, analyze_project, get_code_samples, get_project_structure, get_test_map, get_file_content, get_snapshot, save_snapshot, configure_project.
Core tool implementations — project analysis, code sampling, structure detection, test mapping, and snapshot persistence. Central orchestration module.
Smart file sampler — selects representative code files based on architectural role (config, entry point, viewmodel, repository, service, etc.) with configurable sampling patterns.
Git history analyzer — detects commit conventions (conventional commits, ticket refs), frequently changed files, and stale directories from git log.
Analyzer runner — executes all registered analyzers and collects results with timing.
Snapshot persistence — load/save JSON snapshots of file summaries to .mason/snapshot.json with git hash tracking for staleness detection.
Shared TypeScript types — AnalyzerContext, Finding, AnalyzerResult interfaces.
Git utility — checks if a directory is a git repo.
Simple logging utility with debug/info/warn levels.
CLI bin entry point — imports and runs the CLI.
MCP server bin entry point — creates and starts the MCP server with stdio transport.
Build config — tsup bundler targeting Node 20, ESM output, 3 entry points (mason, mason-mcp, cli).
